extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" holds on all reachable executions. Analysis: (1) The only transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (on send_attempt), guarded by "not opted_out". (2) The only transition that sets opted_out:=true is t1 (on optout), which simultaneously sets sent:=false. (3) No transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so opted_out is monotonic — once true it stays true. Therefore, while opted_out=true: t2 is permanently disabled (its guard requires not opted_out), so sent can never be set to true; meanwhile every other applicable event (t9 on send_attempt, and key transitions t3-t8) either leaves state unchanged or sets sent:=false. Immediately when optout fires (t1), sent is cleared to false. Hence the state (opted_out=true, sent=true) is unreachable, and in every reachable state with opted_out=true, sent is false. No counterexample exists within (or beyond) the 5-event bound. File analyzed: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_hard/_judge_prompts_blind/item-fa22c55c2422.txt
